pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_moodle.sh line 323 creates course with shortname='BIO101'; setup_task.sh line 40 queries for 'BIO101'; export_result.sh line 37 queries for 'BIO101'
Code analysis: task.json task description specifies this name; export_result.sh line 52 searches for '%clinical skills assessment%'; verifier.py line 68 expects this item
Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Maximum grade: 50'; verifier.py line 32-33 expects grade_max=50.0; Moodle documentation confirms maximum grade can be set to any positive value
Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Grade to pass: 30'; verifier.py line 32-33 expects grade_pass=30.0; Moodle documentation confirms grade to pass is a configurable threshold
Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Jane Smith (jsmith): 45'; verifier.py line 34 expects this value; export_result.sh line 78 retrieves grade for 'jsmith'
Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Michael Jones (mjones): 32'; verifier.py line 34 expects this value; export_result.sh line 78 retrieves grade for 'mjones'
Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Alice Wilson (awilson): 48'; verifier.py line 34 expects this value; export_result.sh line 78 retrieves grade for 'awilson'
Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Bob Brown (bbrown): 28'; verifier.py line 34 expects this value; export_result.sh line 78 retrieves grade for 'bbrown'

privileged_info_summary: The task is to create a manual grade item named 'Clinical Skills Assessment' in the BIO101 course (a synthetic test course in a localhost Moodle installation) with maximum grade of 50.0 and passing grade of 30.0. The task requires entering specific grades for four synthetic test students: jsmith (45), mjones (32), awilson (48), and bbrown (28). All metadata values match the task description exactly, and all data is entirely synthetic with no real educational data involved. Moodle is an open-source learning management system that natively supports these gradebook operations.
